description: A modular, extensible, Walberla-inspired LBM-CUDA framework. Target: CFD simulation of Additive Manufacturing processes (DED, LPBF). Revised to strictly follow Walberla patterns, focusing on core LBM and PDE-based thermal solver first.

grid.description: The computational domain is decomposed into a forest of structured blocks, inspired by the walberla::blockforest::BlockForest data structure. This enables efficient parallelization and Adaptive Mesh Refinement (AMR).

indexing.local: Cell-based, (i, j, k) within each block.
indexing.global: Unique ID for each block in the forest.

blocks.description: Each block is a uniform Cartesian grid of cells.

size.notes: Block size is fixed at compile time. Power-of-two sizes are generally optimal for GPU architectures.

communication.notes: Each block is surrounded by layers of ghost cells to exchange data with its neighbors. A depth of 2 is standard for LBM (one for streaming, one for finite difference stencils like gradients).

amr.description: Blocks can be dynamically refined or coarsened based on physical criteria, forming an octree structure.

criteria.notes: Refinement criteria are evaluated periodically to adapt the grid to the evolving simulation.

amr.notes: AMR is a planned feature for a future version (e.g., V2.0) to manage initial implementation complexity.

parallel_partitioning.description: For V1.0, the framework targets a single GPU. The block-based structure is maintained for architectural consistency and future multi-GPU scalability, but inter-process communication is out of scope.

data_structures.description: Core data structures for storing and managing simulation data on the grid.

memory_layout.notes: Structure of Arrays (SoA) is the default for GPU performance, as it facilitates coalesced memory access and vectorization.

fields.description: Fields represent physical quantities stored per cell on the grid. They are the primary data containers.
fields.notes: Fields primarily live on the GPU (device) for computation, but can be mirrored or copied to the CPU (host) for I/O, complex logic, or communication.

types.name: PdfField
types.description: Stores the LBM probability distribution functions (PDFs) for each cell.
types.notes: The core LBM data. Stencil (e.g., D3Q19, D3Q27) determines the number of distributions per cell. Defaulting to single precision for LBM distributions significantly improves performance on GPUs by reducing memory bandwidth and increasing computational throughput. Double precision can be used selectively for other fields where necessary.

types.name: ScalarField
types.description: Stores a single scalar value per cell.

types.name: FlagField
types.description: Stores integer flags per cell to identify its type (e.g., fluid, solid boundary, interface). Inspired by walberla::field::FlagField. Essential for handling multi-phase/solid regions.

flags.name: INTERFACE
flags.description: Marks LIQUID cells adjacent to GAS cells (free surface).

gpu_specifics.description: Details related to GPU data management and performance.
gpu_specifics.device_data_structures: Fields are mapped to pitched, 3D CUDA arrays or linearized memory regions for optimal performance.

memory_management.host_transfer: Use pinned (page-locked) memory for asynchronous Host-to-Device and Device-to-Host transfers via CUDA Streams.

kernel_launch.grid_dim: One MPI rank per GPU, one CUDA grid per simulation.
kernel_launch.block_dim: One CUDA block per grid block (from the BlockForest).
kernel_launch.thread_dim: Threads within a CUDA block map to cells within a grid block.

lbm_models.description: Defines the library of available Lattice Boltzmann models, components, and coupled physics.

options.name: D3Q19
options.description: 19 discrete velocities in 3 dimensions. A standard, robust choice for general-purpose fluid dynamics.

options.name: D3Q27
options.description: 27 discrete velocities in 3 dimensions. Recommended for thermal-hydrodynamic coupling (more accurate temperature gradient) and higher-order models.

options.name: SRT_BGK
options.description: Single-Relaxation-Time Bhatnagar-Gross-Krook. The simplest operator. Recommended starting point for validation before enabling MRT.

options.name: MRT
options.description: Multi-Relaxation-Time. Offers superior stability and accuracy, especially for low-viscosity flows, by relaxing different fluid moments at different rates. Target model, but more complex to implement and debug correctly.

options.name: Cumulant
options.description: An advanced, very stable collision model for high Reynolds number flows, based on a statistical cumulant expansion.

forcing_schemes.description: Methods to incorporate body forces (e.g., gravity, buoyancy, interface forces) into the LBM equation.

options.name: Guo
options.description: A well-established and accurate forcing scheme that correctly recovers the Navier-Stokes equations.

options.name: Shan-Chen
options.description: A popular scheme for simulating multiphase and multicomponent flows by introducing a pseudo-potential-based inter-particle interaction force.

thermal_models.description: Models for handling heat transport and its coupling with the fluid flow. Actual implementation within ThermalSweepPDE.

numerics.description: Defines the numerical execution flow, inspired by Walberla's sweep-based architecture.

timeloop.description: The main loop iterates through a sequence of 'sweeps' at each time step.

dt.notes: Time step 'dt' is typically linked to the grid spacing 'dx' in LBM (dt=dx in lattice units). Must satisfy stability requirements.

thermal_solver_scheme.notes: Time integration scheme used within ThermalSweepPDE. Explicit is simpler to start, implicit (Crank-Nicolson) preferred for stability later.

sweeps.description: Self-contained computational kernels executed sequentially by the timeloop.

library.name: UpdateBoundaries
library.description: Applies boundary conditions to LBM PDFs and thermal fields. Must read FlagField.

library.name: ComputeMacroscopicQuantities
library.description: Calculates density and velocity from LBM PDFs. Required before collision.

library.name: ThermalSweepPDE
library.description: Solves the enthalpy conservation equation using a Finite Difference/Volume approach, inspired by Walberla's PDE solver framework (`src/pde/`). Handles conduction in solid/liquid/gas, phase change (enthalpy method), and conjugate heat transfer. Must read FlagField for phase-dependent properties and velocity for advection. Updates Temperature and LiquidFraction fields.

library.name: ApplyForcing
library.description: Applies body forces: buoyancy (Boussinesq), solidification drag (porous medium based on liquid fraction and FlagField). Future: Will incorporate interface forces. Uses the selected LBM forcing scheme (e.g., Guo). Must read FlagField to apply drag correctly.

library.name: Collision
library.description: Performs the LBM collision step (e.g., MRT or BGK) on the PdfField. Must read FlagField to skip SOLID cells.

library.name: Streaming
library.description: Performs LBM streaming (Pull scheme). Must read FlagField to apply BounceBack at SOLID boundaries.

library.name: PostStepDiagnostics
library.description: Optional: computes global quantities, checks stability (NaN/inf), logs progress.

lbm_options.name: BounceBack
lbm_options.description: Simple no-slip wall. Applied during Streaming based on FlagField.

lbm_options.name: Periodic
lbm_options.description: Connects opposite sides of the domain.

nonlinear_solver.description: Iterative algorithm for nonlinear physics (e.g., temperature-dependent properties) within ThermalSweepPDE.

governing_equations.fluid_dynamics: LBM (e.g., D3Q19 MRT) solves weakly-compressible Navier-Stokes.

heat_transport.description: Enthalpy conservation equation solved by ThermalSweepPDE.
heat_transport.notes: Solved via FDM/FVM within ThermalSweepPDE, inspired by Walberla's PDE framework.

coupling.description: Couples thermal and fluid fields via buoyancy.

phase_change_model.description: Enthalpy-based model for solid-liquid phase change.
phase_change_model.notes: Implemented within ThermalSweepPDE. Temperature T and liquid fraction f_l are derived from the solved enthalpy H.

solidification_model.notes: Treats mushy zone as porous medium. Applied in 'ApplyForcing' sweep based on f_l from ThermalSweepPDE and FlagField (not applied in GAS).

laser_source_model.notes: Implemented either as a dedicated LaserSourceSweep or integrated within ThermalSweepPDE.

material_properties.description: Phase-dependent and potentially temperature-dependent material properties.
material_properties.notes: Accessed by ThermalSweepPDE based on FlagField and Temperature.

interface_physics.description: Handles free surface tracking and associated physics (surface tension, Marangoni). Target: Implement using Walberla's preferred approach (likely Level Set based or integrated within PDE framework). Details TBD after core LBM and Thermal solver validation.

io_and_monitoring.description: Defines standards for configuration, I/O, and monitoring.

configuration.schema: A single JSON file defines the simulation setup.

configuration_file.description: Single JSON instance of this blueprint drives simulation.

python_api.description: High-level Python interface inspired by walberla::python_coupling.

plugin_interfaces.description: Inherit from base classes to add custom components.

components.name: CustomMaterialProperty
components.description: Provide C++ functor or Python callable for complex properties.

code_generation.description: Code generation for optimized kernels (like Walberla's `src/lbm_generated`) is a planned future optimization, to be implemented after core physics are stable.

linear_system_solver.description: Solvers for sparse linear systems from implicit thermal discretization.

options.name: AMG
options.description: Most powerful/scalable (via external libs like AmgX/hypre).
